questions.question: What is TypeScript?
options: B) A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to JavaScript
questions.correctAnswer: B) A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to JavaScript

questions.question: What does TypeScript type inference do?
options: B) Infers variable or expression types when explicit annotations are omitted
questions.correctAnswer: B) Infers variable or expression types when explicit annotations are omitted

questions.question: What is the difference between interface and type alias in TypeScript?
options: B) Interfaces are primarily for object shapes and can merge; type aliases can represent unions, primitives, tuples, and more
C) Type aliases support merging but interfaces do not
questions.correctAnswer: B) Interfaces are primarily for object shapes and can merge; type aliases can represent unions, primitives, tuples, and more

questions.question: What does a union type like string | number mean?
options: A) Value must be both string and number
B) Value can be either string or number
questions.correctAnswer: B) Value can be either string or number

questions.question: What does the unknown type enforce compared to any?
options: A) It behaves exactly like any
B) It requires narrowing or assertion before unsafe operations
C) It cannot hold any value
D) It is only for arrays
questions.correctAnswer: B) It requires narrowing or assertion before unsafe operations

questions.question: What is the never type used for?
options: A) Variables that are always null
B) Values that never occur, such as functions that always throw or impossible union branches
questions.correctAnswer: B) Values that never occur, such as functions that always throw or impossible union branches

questions.question: What problem do generics solve?
options: B) Writing reusable code while preserving type information
questions.correctAnswer: B) Writing reusable code while preserving type information

questions.question: What does keyof T produce?
options: A) Runtime values of object T
B) A union of property names of type T
questions.correctAnswer: B) A union of property names of type T

questions.question: What is a discriminated union?
options: B) Union of object types sharing a common literal field used for narrowing
C) A union that cannot be narrowed
questions.correctAnswer: B) Union of object types sharing a common literal field used for narrowing

questions.question: What is the purpose of type guards?
options: B) To narrow broader types into more specific ones safely in control flow
questions.correctAnswer: B) To narrow broader types into more specific ones safely in control flow

questions.question: What does Partial<T> do?
options: B) Makes all properties of T optional
questions.correctAnswer: B) Makes all properties of T optional

questions.question: What does Record<K, V> represent?
options: B) An object type whose keys are K and whose values are V
questions.correctAnswer: B) An object type whose keys are K and whose values are V

questions.question: What does tsconfig.json control?
options: B) TypeScript compiler options such as target, module, strictness, and included files
questions.correctAnswer: B) TypeScript compiler options such as target, module, strictness, and included files

questions.question: What does strict mode in TypeScript improve?
options: B) Type safety by enabling stricter compiler checks
questions.correctAnswer: B) Type safety by enabling stricter compiler checks

questions.question: Why are declaration files (.d.ts) useful?
options: B) They describe type information for JavaScript libraries without TS source
questions.correctAnswer: B) They describe type information for JavaScript libraries without TS source

questions.question: What is indexed access type T[K]?
options: B) A type-level way to access the type of property K on T
questions.correctAnswer: B) A type-level way to access the type of property K on T

questions.question: What is the purpose of extends in a generic constraint like <T extends { id: string }>?
options: B) Restricting T to types compatible with the required shape
questions.correctAnswer: B) Restricting T to types compatible with the required shape

questions.question: Why is any considered dangerous?
options: B) It disables type checking and can hide bugs that TypeScript would otherwise catch
D) It only works in classes
questions.correctAnswer: B) It disables type checking and can hide bugs that TypeScript would otherwise catch

questions.question: What is exhaustive checking in a switch over a discriminated union?
options: B) Ensuring every possible variant is handled, often using never in the default branch
questions.correctAnswer: B) Ensuring every possible variant is handled, often using never in the default branch

questions.question: What is the difference between compile-time types and runtime values in TypeScript?
options: B) Types are erased during compilation and do not exist at runtime unless represented by actual JS values
C) Types run before values in the browser
questions.correctAnswer: B) Types are erased during compilation and do not exist at runtime unless represented by actual JS values
